title: Public ACLs or policies on S3 bucket
description: Detects if an S3 bucket is configured with public ACLs or policies that allow public access.
message: Public ACLs or policies detected on S3 bucket
impact: Exposure of data to unauthorized users
recommendation: Ensure S3 buckets do not use public ACLs or public policies such as 'PublicRead' or 'PublicReadWrite'.

noncompliant.code:
import boto3

s3 = boto3.client('s3')

s3.put_bucket_acl(Bucket='my-bucket', ACL='public-read')  # ❌ Public access
s3.put_bucket_policy(Bucket='my-bucket', Policy='{"Statement": [{"Effect": "Allow", "Principal": "*"}]}')  # ❌ Public policy

compliant.code:
import boto3

s3 = boto3.client('s3')

s3.put_bucket_acl(Bucket='my-bucket', ACL='private')  # ✅ Private access
s3.put_bucket_policy(Bucket='my-bucket', Policy='{"Statement": [{"Effect": "Allow", "Principal": {"AWS": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/SpecificUser"}}]}')  # ✅ Restricted policy

why_is_this_an_issue: Public ACLs or policies on S3 buckets expose sensitive data to anyone on the internet.
how_to_fix: Use 'private' or restricted IAM-based access policies for all S3 buckets.
